acceptedAnswer.text: taiwan-stock-radar is an open-source Taiwan stock market research and ranking system for AI agents, analysts, and developers. It combines market sweep, top 20 ranking, and single-stock deep-dive analysis.

mainEntity.name: How does taiwan-stock-radar create a top 20 stock list?

acceptedAnswer.text: It first scans a broad Taiwan stock universe, evaluates market regime, sector rotation, factor strength, and risk filters, then ranks candidates and surfaces the top 20 stocks that deserve closer attention.

mainEntity.name: Does a high score mean a stock will definitely go up?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. A high score means the stock currently has stronger supporting conditions in the framework. It is a ranking and research signal, not a guarantee of future returns.

mainEntity.name: How are buy and sell points determined?

acceptedAnswer.text: The workflow frames action zones instead of fixed promises. It identifies a preferred buy zone, invalidation or stop zone, and one or more take-profit or trim zones based on trend structure, volatility, and risk rules.

mainEntity.name: What happens when a user asks about one specific stock?

acceptedAnswer.text: The system switches into a deeper research mode. It adds peer comparison, business and catalyst context, market support or resistance, scenario trees, and a more explicit action plan for that stock.
